meta.title: Velar Engines — The Complete UK Guide

hero.h1: Velar Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine. Every known failure. Every replacement cost — including why "D300" means two different engines on this car. Backed by 275+ real UK Velar enquiries [EM-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ✅ Usually Worth Replacing — £3,500–£9,500 recons [EM-QUOTE] against £18,000–£60,000 host values clear our 60% rule comfortably across the range
★★★☆☆ — engine choice decides it: the petrols and post-2021 sixes are strong; the pre-2019 diesel four and V6-era D300 carry the known JLR caveats
2017–2020 D300 (306DT V6) — crankshaft bearing • pre-2019 204DTD — timing chain
2021+ facelift P400 or D300 — Ingenium sixes, Pivi Pro, no legacy engines
"D300" = 306DT V6 (2017–2020, crank-bearing risk) OR AJ20D6 straight-six (2021+, clean record). Same badge, opposite stories — check the year before the price.
🎨 The Design Pick — bought on looks, owned on engine choice

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The Velar is the Range Rover people choose with their eyes — our job is to make sure the engine choice is made with the data, because under one body sit eight powertrains ranging from near-faultless to fully caveated.

generations.subHeadline: Every Velar is an L560. The decision that matters isn't which generation — it's which engine.

cards.title: Range Rover Velar (L560)
cards.verdict: Buy on the 2021 facelift line and the engine, in that order. Post-2021 cars swap every caveated engine for a clean one — the rare facelift that changed the mechanicals more than the face.

cards.title: Which Velar Powertrain Is Right for You?

engineDatabase.h2: Velar Engine Codes — The Complete Database

engines.enquiries: 120 Velar D300 cohort [EM-VERIFIED — spans both D300 engines; split at source]

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: 204DTD and 306DT ratings: 93% confidence — high-volume engines in our dataset [EM-VERIFIED] with 275 Velar-specific enquiries behind the model-level calls. AJ126, P550 and post-2021 sixes: Expert-knowledge / Provisional tier — smaller or younger parcs, rated on documented patterns and shared-architecture evidence. Data note (internal): Correction applied: the source sheet listed the Velar's engines as "204DTD, 204PT, 306DT (D300)" only — added the petrols it omitted (AJ126 P380, AJ133 P550 SVAutobiography Dynamic) and the full 2021+ facelift slate (AJ20D6, AJ20P6, P400e). Action needed: the 120 "L560 D300" enquiries [EM-VERIFIED] span two different engines (306DT to 2020, AJ20D6 from 2021) — split by registration year at source, because right now a clean engine and a caveated one share a single statistic.

problems.description: The pre-2021 D300 carries the same bottom-end risk as its Discovery 4 and Range Rover Sport siblings — cold-start knock is the warning. £6,500–£9,500 recon [EM-QUOTE]. 2017–2020 cars only.
Pre-2019 Ingenium diesel chain wear — cold-start rattle is the tell. Improved from 2019. Affects early D180/D240.
The style-buy problem: Velars bought for the school run interrupt DPF regens, fuel dilutes the oil, wear accelerates. Urban drivers belong in the petrols or PHEV.
Coolant loss and rough running on higher-mileage Ingenium diesels — repairable if caught before overheating.
The P380's supercharger nose and cooling-system age points — a specialist check item on any pre-facelift performance car.
Not an engine failure, but the Velar's most common non-engine enquiry — parasitic drain and infotainment glitches on pre-Pivi cars, occasionally misread as engine faults.

marketIntelligence.h2: Velar Market Intelligence — What 275 UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.demandTrend: ⬆ Rising as the 2017–2018 parc exits warranty [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]

insights: Interesting insight: Nearly half of all Velar engine enquiries name one variant — the D300 [EM-VERIFIED]. No other JLR model concentrates its engine anxiety on a single badge like this, and the badge covers two different engines. That confusion is the Velar engine market.

Velar enquiries skew later-mileage and diesel-heavy relative to the petrol-rich parc — consistent with the diesels doing the working miles while the petrols do the style miles. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)
Post-2021 cars barely register in failure enquiries yet — early evidence the facelift's clean engine slate is holding. (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every Velar Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one Velar based on this page, buy a 2021-or-later car — and remember that 'D300' means two different engines on this model. The one with the crankshaft question retired in 2020; make sure the listing you're reading knows that too.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: 204DTD, 306DT, 204PT, AJ20D6 figures [EM-QUOTE]; AJ126, AJ133 and AJ20P6 rows [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs Table A].
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: 10–20 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [EM-QUOTE]. The Velar rule: With values of £18,000–£60,000, replacement clears our 60% scrap-threshold rule across essentially the whole parc — ✅ Safe. The one pressure point is the earliest D180s heading toward £15,000: a worst-case £5,800 recon on a tired one is still comfortably inside the line, but a V6 D300 quote against a cheap early car is the maths to run before you commit.

economicsBox.title: The Velar rule
economicsBox.text: With values of £18,000–£60,000, replacement clears our 60% scrap-threshold rule across essentially the whole parc — ✅ Safe. The one pressure point is the earliest D180s heading toward £15,000: a worst-case £5,800 recon on a tired one is still comfortably inside the line, but a V6 D300 quote against a cheap early car is the maths to run before you commit.

engineEvolution.h2: How the Velar Engine Range Evolved — 2017 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: The Velar launched as the design flagship on borrowed engines — the Ingenium fours plus the ageing V6s from across the range
eras.worthKnowing: It inherited every engine's existing reputation on day one, good and bad

eras.whyBmwChanged: A short-run 5.0 V8 halo car — the SVAutobiography Dynamic
eras.worthKnowing: Rare, collectible, and a supercharger-history purchase

eras.whyBmwChanged: Electrification retired the V6s — the straight-sixes and PHEV arrived with Pivi Pro
eras.worthKnowing: The rare facelift where the biggest changes were under the bonnet: every caveated engine left the range at once

profiles.verdict: Five seats, no more — it's the style choice, not the space one. If you need seven, the Discovery costs the same used.
The P250 is the honest commuter Velar; the P400e is the company-car sweet spot. Diesel commuting under 15 miles a day is DPF roulette.
2,400–2,500kg braked on the sixes — capable, but this body was styled, not built, for the yard. D300 (either era) is the tow pick.
Early D180s are the affordable entry — budget a chain inspection before purchase and they're a sound buy. Cheap pre-2021 D300s: run the crank maths first.
The reason this car exists. Buy the 2021+ facelift, pick the engine with the table above, and enjoy the best-looking Range Rover of its era with a clean mechanical conscience.

items.question: How much does a Velar engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Reconditioned units run £3,000–£9,500 supply only depending on engine [EM-QUOTE] — the volume 204DTD at £3,500–£5,800, either D300 at £6,500–£9,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,900. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

items.question: Which Velar engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The 204PT petrol has the cleanest record in our data, with the post-2021 AJ20D6 and AJ20P6 sixes close behind on early evidence.

items.question: Which Velar engine should I avoid?
items.answer: A 2017–2020 D300 (306DT V6) without documented oil-interval history — it carries the same crankshaft-bearing risk as its Discovery and Range Rover Sport siblings. A pre-2019 D180 without a chain inspection is the other caveat.

items.question: Is the Velar D300 reliable?
items.answer: Which one? The 2021+ D300 (AJ20D6 straight-six) — yes, genuinely. The 2017–2020 D300 (306DT V6) — only with maintenance history, because of the bottom-end risk. Same badge, two engines. Full explainer →

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Velar engine?
items.answer: Almost always — Velar values comfortably clear our 60% scrap-threshold rule against every recon cost in the range. The only maths worth running twice is a V6 D300 quote against the cheapest early cars. Use the Ownership Decision Tool for your car.

items.question: Which Velar should I buy?
items.answer: A 2021-or-later facelift — every caveated engine left the range at once, and Pivi Pro fixed the infotainment complaints in the same stroke. Use our powertrain chooser above to pick the engine for your miles.

items.question: How do I find my Velar engine code?
items.answer: On the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. On this model the year does most of the work: D300 before 2021 = 306DT V6; from 2021 = AJ20D6. Or tell us your variant and year and we'll identify it.

items.question: Velar vs Range Rover Sport — which should I buy?
items.answer: The Velar is the design pick; the Sport is the capability pick, with more towing, more engines and (used) more risk exposure via the SDV6. Same money buys a newer Velar or an older Sport — on engine risk alone, the newer Velar usually wins. Read the full head-to-head →
